dashboard_meta.architecture: Layer 1 agents are sealed independent raw signal producers. No cross-agent communication.

agents.summary: USD Layer 1 raw call generated from sealed independent workflow.

24h.reason: USD shows bullish bias driven by strong Fed hawkish bias, positive US 2Y yield delta, and positive US economic surprise. However, narrowing US/Germany 2Y spread and bearish DXY and VIX signals conflict, limiting confidence. High active participation with mixed primary driver signals results in bullish lean bias for most timeframes, with full bullish only in the current week where rate and trend agreement is stronger.

F1 VIX.reason: Risk-on conditions reduce USD safe-haven demand

F8 Fed Bias.reason: Hawkish Fed bias moves support USD

F5 DXY 5-Day Delta.reason: DXY down more than 0.30% over 5 days indicates USD weakness

F6 Gold 5-Day Delta.reason: No directional signal from gold price move

F2 US 2Y Yield Delta.reason: US 2Y rising more than 5 bps over 5 days supports USD
F2 US 2Y Yield Delta.evidence: US 2Y yield rose 6 bps over 5 days

F9 Dollar Smile Regime.reason: Moderate US/global growth with hawkish Fed generates bearish Dollar Smile signal
F9 Dollar Smile Regime.evidence: VIX below 16, equities in risk_on regime, Fed hawkish but growth moderate

F3 US/DE 2Y Spread Delta.reason: Narrowing spread reduces USD support versus Germany
F3 US/DE 2Y Spread Delta.evidence: US/DE spread narrowed by 5 bps over 5 days

F4 US 10Y Real Yield Delta.reason: Rise at threshold; counted as neutral due to strict rule
F4 US 10Y Real Yield Delta.evidence: US 10Y real yield rose 5 bps exactly over 5 days

F10 Equity Direction vs USD Correlation.reason: Equities falling in risk-on supports USD
F10 Equity Direction vs USD Correlation.evidence: Risk-on regime with equities falling (NQ down 4.8%)

key_factors: F1 VIX: BEARISH — VIX 15.4, d1 -0.66, d5 -0.34 (Risk-on conditions reduce USD safe-haven demand)
F8 Fed Bias: BULLISH — Fed bias is hawkish (Hawkish Fed bias moves support USD)
F5 DXY 5-Day Delta: BEARISH — DXY fell 0.41% over 5 days (DXY down more than 0.30% over 5 days indicates USD weakness)
F6 Gold 5-Day Delta: NEUTRAL — Gold flat over 5 days (No directional signal from gold price move)
F2 US 2Y Yield Delta: BULLISH — US 2Y yield rose 6 bps over 5 days (US 2Y rising more than 5 bps over 5 days supports USD)
F9 Dollar Smile Regime: BEARISH — VIX below 16, equities in risk_on regime, Fed hawkish but growth moderate (Moderate US/global growth with hawkish Fed generates bearish Dollar Smile signal)
F7 US Economic Surprise: BULLISH — Recent US data (NFP) positive surprise (Positive economic surprise supports USD)
F3 US/DE 2Y Spread Delta: BEARISH — US/DE spread narrowed by 5 bps over 5 days (Narrowing spread reduces USD support versus Germany)
F4 US 10Y Real Yield Delta: NEUTRAL — US 10Y real yield rose 5 bps exactly over 5 days (Rise at threshold; counted as neutral due to strict rule)
F10 Equity Direction vs USD Correlation: BULLISH — Risk-on regime with equities falling (NQ down 4.8%) (Equities falling in risk-on supports USD)
warnings: Primary driver conflict between Fed bias and US-Germany spread
Bearish VIX and DXY signals reduce conviction
Moderate Gold and 10Y real yield signals neutral
agents.reasoning_summary: USD shows bullish bias driven by strong Fed hawkish bias, positive US 2Y yield delta, and positive US economic surprise. However, narrowing US/Germany 2Y spread and bearish DXY and VIX signals conflict, limiting confidence. High active participation with mixed primary driver signals results in bullish lean bias for most timeframes, with full bullish only in the current week where rate and trend agreement is stronger.

conviction_model.final_conviction_logic: After applying conflict penalty (Fed bias conflicts with US-DE spread and DXY signals), final conviction rounded to 66%. High active weight but primary driver conflict leads to lean verdicts in most timeframes.
